action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 13 failed immediately (4.8 sec) due to TypeError: ReduceLROnPlateau does not accept verbose=True in this PyTorch version. The previous best eval score (rmse_z500=1018.63) was from iteration 5 and its checkpoint was overwritten. We have ~50 minutes remaining. Analysis: We need to retrain the proven architecture (ResNet hidden=256, 12 blocks, no residual) that produced the best score. With batch_size=128, 1 epoch takes ~187 sec. Training for up to 10 epochs (~31 min) plus eval (~3 min) fits comfortably within the remaining time. Decision: Rewrite train.py to remove the verbose keyword, keep the exact architecture and training config that worked, set max_epochs=10 with early stopping patience=5, and save to a new path (checkpoints_retrain/best_retrained.pt). Run training now and submit eval in the next iteration.
